original_tool_input.base_case: An intact mechanical bull trend (50d > 200d, price +7.9% above 200dma) is now in a confirmed, controlled correction off the June 2 ATH. Base case over 30-60 days is continued downside testing toward the 23.6% Fib (~7,553 per context / ~7,330 per report) and likely the 38.2% Fib primary re-entry zone, with the 61.8%/SMA-50 convergence (~6,860) as the maximum-conviction add. No bottom confirmed yet; VIX has not produced a capitulation spike.
original_tool_input.trend_regime: 50-day SMA (7,174.5) above 200-day SMA (6,863.3); price 7,405.7 above both. Price +3.2% above 50d, +7.9% above 200d. Mechanically intact bullish regime but maturing — 50d premium compressing as price corrects from ATH.
original_tool_input.valuation_bucket: Forward PE 20.92x (cautious buying only at strong confluence band, 20-22x); ERP +0.23% sits in the 0.0-0.5% valuation-ceiling band (trim bias, no aggressive adds). ERP-confirmed re-entry floor at 0.5% sits near 7,007.

signals.bollinger_position: below upper band, pressing toward middle/20-day

what_changed_today: Regime arc held at Late Bull / Topping for a 5th logged session.
Nominal green close (+0.30%) masks a 2nd consecutive distribution session: open near high (~7,466), sold off all day, closed in bottom ~15% of range.
McClellan breadth downgraded from Fear to EXTREME FEAR (~990) — fastest breadth deterioration since the May shock.
VIX eased from ~21.5 to 18.92, back below the 20 framework threshold — no capitulation spike yet.
Fear & Greed fell to 40 (Fear), a 9-session collapse from 61.
Put/Call rose from ~0.60 to ~0.68 — first constructive contrarian shift in 10 sessions (watch, not action).
ERP improved to ~+0.23% from the 0.097% June 2 cycle low but remains in the valuation-ceiling band.
Monte Carlo flipped decisively bearish: prob_down_first 79% — active leg fully retraced and downside re-anchored to liquidation_caution 7,392.
original_tool_input.narrative_summary: The market remains Late Bull / Topping with a mechanically intact trend now in a confirmed, controlled correction: a flattering green close hides a second straight open-high-to-close-near-low distribution session, breadth has been downgraded to Extreme Fear, and junk spreads sit at April-shock levels for a fifth day. ERP has improved to ~0.23% and the put/call is finally ticking up, but neither clears the bar for action, and the Monte Carlo edge is decisively to the downside (79% down-first) below all thresholds. The defining absence is a VIX capitulation spike — every prior cycle bottom came with one, and VIX has eased back below 20 rather than spiking. Posture is defensive patience: hold, do not chase, and stage re-entries to the 38.2% Fib and the 61.8%/SMA-50 convergence on confirmation.
open_questions: Will the 23.6% Fib / liquidation-caution zone (~7,392-7,553) hold as support or fail cleanly toward the 38.2% Fib?
Will junk-bond spreads (5th day at ~1.45%+) finally tighten or sustain wide, confirming a credit warning?
Does the put/call uptick mark the early-bottoming sequence (typically 2-5 sessions ahead), or is it premature?
Will a VIX capitulation spike into the 25-30 zone materialize to validate a max-conviction re-entry?

RSI-14 ~35 and declining toward oversold; 3-month MFI mid-range (corrective, not structural)
Price pressing toward/below 20-day after extended advance — regime test underway
Off upper band, testing middle band
Junk spreads ~1.45%+ at April-shock levels for 5th consecutive session
McClellan downgraded to Extreme Fear (~990); stock price strength weakening
18.92 — eased back below 20; standard regime, no capitulation spike
Close at liquidation-caution (7,392); nervous zone 7,240, margin-call 6,859

Hold full position, no chase; stage 38.2% Fib primary re-entry and 61.8%/SMA-50 max-conviction add; require 3 of 4 confirmation signals before deploying

confirming_evidence: Second consecutive distribution session: open near high, close in bottom ~15% of range despite nominal green print.
McClellan breadth downgraded to Extreme Fear (~990), worst since the May shock — internals worse than price.
Junk-bond spreads at April-shock levels (~1.45%+) for a 5th consecutive session — sustained credit warning.
Monte Carlo decisively bearish: 79% down-first, active leg fully retraced, downside re-anchored to liquidation-caution; below all thresholds.
Fear & Greed at 40 (Fear), a 9-session collapse from 61 — most rapid sustained fade since Oct 2025.
Forward PE 20.92x and ERP +0.23% in valuation-ceiling band — no fundamental cushion for aggressive adds.

conflicting_evidence.bullish_read: 50d > 200d, price above both SMAs and +7.9% over 200d — mechanically intact bull regime.
conflicting_evidence.bearish_read: Two consecutive open-high-to-close-near-low distribution sessions and Extreme Fear breadth signal a topping process beneath the intact skeleton.
conflicting_evidence.framework_rule: A close below the 20-day SMA after an extended advance is a regime shift, not just a normal dip.

conflicting_evidence.bullish_read: Index still above 50d and 200d; nominal close was green.
conflicting_evidence.bearish_read: When breadth (Extreme Fear McClellan) and credit (5th day wide spreads) both diverge from price, elevate caution even before price breaks down.
conflicting_evidence.framework_rule: When breadth and credit both diverge from price, elevate caution even if price has not yet broken down.

conflicting_evidence.bullish_read: Put/Call rose 0.60 -> 0.68, first constructive hedging shift in 10 sessions — early bottoming watch.
conflicting_evidence.bearish_read: VIX eased back below 20 with no capitulation spike into 25-30; correction is orderly, not capitulative, so no high-quality re-entry trigger yet.
conflicting_evidence.framework_rule: A VIX spike into 25-30+ followed by reversal is high-quality re-entry; a correction without that spike is orderly, not capitulative.

conflicting_evidence.bullish_read: ERP improving to +0.23% from the 0.097% cycle low — trend moving the right way.
conflicting_evidence.bearish_read: ERP still in the 0.0-0.5% valuation-ceiling band; re-entry floor near 7,007 implies meaningful further downside needed for true value.
conflicting_evidence.framework_rule: ERP 0.0-0.5% = valuation ceiling, trim bias, no aggressive adds.

original_tool_input.primary_tension: A mechanically intact bull skeleton (50d > 200d, price above both SMAs) is colliding with a confirmed two-day distribution top, Extreme Fear breadth, a 5-day credit warning, and a decisively bearish Monte Carlo edge (79% down-first) — while the absence of a VIX capitulation spike and a first put/call uptick keep any re-entry premature.

repaired_signals.bollinger_position: below upper band, pressing toward middle/20-day
